title: [Bug]: NATS consumer normalization accepts negative JetStream metadata values
summary: The NATS message adapter converts negative sequence and pending metadata values into envelope fields instead of treating them as absent.
observed: A message-like object with negative JetStream sequence or pending values produces an envelope containing those negative values.
expected: Negative JetStream sequence, delivery, and pending values should be ignored during normalization because they are not valid operational metadata.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that normalizes a message-like object with negative metadata values.
impact: Corrupted or hostile metadata could flow into idempotency keys, routing decisions, metrics, or persisted metadata.
delivery_semantics: The fix treats invalid metadata as missing and avoids creating invalid idempotency keys from negative values.
security: The adapter continues treating client-provided metadata as untrusted input at the boundary.
acceptance: Negative JetStream metadata values normalize to missing values.
Positive JetStream metadata values continue to normalize correctly.
Consumer and envelope tests continue to pass.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, envelope tests, commit-then-ack tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update release notes if metadata normalization hardening is mentioned.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
